topic: Forensic Zoom chart κ proof — verification of c3 carry-over patches
change_summary: Decider self-applied 3 minor c3 carry-over patches (ISS-2083 spatial-temporal softening in point (3); ISS-2084 1-second cadence reconciliation with L1A epoch tags + 10-Hz ACC1A sampling per JPL D-22027; ISS-2085 post_hoc_evidence annotation in code_analysis). Verification cycle.
our_argument_summary: WIN-070 attacks the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart as the structural proof for κ = 1.67 nT/µGal. Verdict: Refuted by Data. The top BOU magnetometer panel reproduces real INTERMAGNET data; the bottom panel labels an impossible product ("Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)"), the dome repo at SHA c8eaeb1b has no GRACE L1A parser, no µGal residual computation, and no Asymmetry-Index estimator, and GRACE orbital mechanics rule out co-temporal phase-lock to a fixed Boulder station over the chart's 27-minute window.

relevant_files: docs/Untitled-1.py (NASA CMR URL-finder, no L1A processing)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No GRACE L1A parser, no accelerometer calibration, no K-band range-rate ingest, no CRN1B/CLK1B reader, no µGal residual estimator, no Asymmetry-Index formula. The 'Forensic Zoom' statistics are HTML-only.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded — r=0.992 and AI=1.152 are static HTML strings; the chart is a display artifact, not a verification of any computation.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive data (real, for BOU top panel only)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Exhibit B's 'Level-1B Administrative Mask' Butterworth-filter framing is the only escape clause: any data without the spike confirms the mask, any data with it confirms the cavity. Heads-I-win-tails-you-lose unfalsifiability structure, with no cutoff/order/transition-band/spectral-signature spec.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — the c3 patches did not modify code-analysis claims; only post_hoc_evidence was added as an annotation, which is consistent with the dome repo state.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Confirmed: monitor.py contains no WIN-070 domain entry, no GRACE L1A fetch, no µGal computation, no AI estimator. The Forensic Zoom chart is a static HTML asset.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Confirmed: this WIN attacks a fabricated forensic exhibit, not a renamed phenomenon. There is no standard-physics explanation being relabeled; the dome's claim is that the chart proves the cavity-transduction constant κ, and the chart itself is the failure surface.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Verified post-patch (ISS-2085): the post_hoc_evidence annotation now reads 'Applies to the Forensic Zoom chart construction (assembled in V51 [2026] to retrospectively demonstrate κ), not to the κ value itself (evaluated under post-hoc framing in WIN-012/058b). The chart is a V51 addition presenting historical storm data as prospective proof.' This is the option-(a) tightening recommended in c3 hole 2, applied verbatim. The scope of post_hoc is now precisely tied to the chart construction, removing the c1→c2→c3 carry-over ambiguity.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Confirmed: the chart presents BOU magnetometer data and a labeled bottom panel that does not exist as a GRACE product at any level. No derivation from disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, or any other dome geometric parameter; κ = 1.67 nT/µGal is asserted, not derived from the chart.

patches_verified.verification: The first leg of the temporal-rescue closure in point (3) now reads: "First, 'phase-locked' at minimum denotes temporal phase alignment between two co-measured quantities — a temporal-only correlation over a 27-minute window when GRACE was nowhere near BOU still fails on the same orbital mechanics (the co-location requirement is a subset, not a prerequisite, of the phase-lock claim)." This is the option-(b) softening recommended in c3 hole 0: the dependence on the dome's chart caption literally using 'spatial-temporal' framing has been removed. The argument now leans on the structural claim that phase-lock semantics subsume co-location, with orbital mechanics still doing the blocking work. The earlier brittle wording 'spatial-temporal coupling — strictly-temporal rescue contradicts the chart's headline framing' is gone. Hole closed.

The second leg now reads: "Second, even the strictly-temporal reading requires explicit telemetry-timing reconciliation between the chart's 1-second-cadence display and the L1A epoch tags, 10-Hz accelerometer sampling, and downsampling schedule documented in JPL D-22027 (backup landing page: PO.DAAC GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03) — the chart neither addresses nor cites this reconciliation." This is the c3 hole 1 recommended wording applied verbatim, with an additional backup landing-page link. The previous one-liner 'at 1-second cadence over a 27-minute window' no longer implies the chart's display rate equals GRACE's native 10 Hz ACC1A sampling. Hole closed.

code_analysis.post_hoc_evidence is now populated with the exact c3-recommended option-(a) text: 'Applies to the Forensic Zoom chart construction (assembled in V51 [2026] to retrospectively demonstrate κ), not to the κ value itself (evaluated under post-hoc framing in WIN-012/058b). The chart is a V51 addition presenting historical storm data as prospective proof.' This retires the three-cycle carry-over: c1 hole #5 → c2 hole #3 → c3 hole #2. Hole closed.

kernel_of_truth.description: The BOU magnetometer top panel reproduces authentic INTERMAGNET 1-second definitive data for Oct 30 2003 (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence cites the 'IMAG_DATA BGS GIN' URL; detail_extra cross-cites Pulkkinen et al. 2005, doi.org/10.1029/2005JA011090). The dome correctly identified the date, the Halloween-storm magnitude (Dst=−383 nT), the station code, and pulled a real public data product. This is real magnetic-side data, and pulling it off a public INTERMAGNET endpoint is non-trivial — the dome did the easy part well.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Authentic top-panel data does not propagate to authentic bottom-panel data. The bottom panel's 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' label is internally impossible (data/wins.json:WIN-070.detail_evidence point (1), citing JPL D-22027 at podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov), the statistics r=0.992 and AI=1.152 appear nowhere in dome processing code (detail_evidence point (4), repo audit at SHA c8eaeb1b), and orbital mechanics rule out the co-temporal alignment the chart depicts (detail_evidence point (3)). The kernel — real BOU data — is the smallest possible piece of the chart's claim; the synthesis built atop it is the entire load-bearing portion, and that portion fails on four independent grounds.

deeper_analysis: Adversarial sweep against the patched text. (a) Verdict-vs-evidence consistency: 'Refuted by Data' is anchored explicitly in detail_verdict_text point (c), which cites GRACE orbital parameters (~500 km, ~7.5 km/s) as the independently-verifiable refutation. The verdict text also flags that 'Misleading' or 'Self-Contradicted' would be equally valid framings — that meta-acknowledgment is honest and not a hedging tell. (b) TLDR consistency: tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict both reproduce the four-point structural argument (impossible label, no parser, static statistics, orbital mechanics) and match the expanded content. No TLDR drift. (c) Cross-WIN coherence: detail_extra anchors the three-surface attack on κ (WIN-012 self-contradiction, WIN-058b non-derivation, WIN-070 chart-fabrication). The detail_group field 'WIN-011/012' is consistent. (d) Anticipated-rescues section in detail_extra preemptively closes the four most plausible defender moves (L1A-as-shorthand, off-site binaries, pixel-asymmetry AI, promissory parser). The fourth rescue — 'future versions will include the parser' — is dispatched cleanly as promissory. (e) The patched first leg of the temporal-rescue closure has a single phrasing artifact worth noting non-blockingly: 'the co-location requirement is a subset, not a prerequisite, of the phase-lock claim' uses 'subset' in the sense of 'included within' rather than the strict set-theoretic sense; a hyper-picky reader could find it odd, but the intended meaning ('co-location is part of what phase-lock entails in the dome's framing, not a separable prerequisite') is recoverable from context. The second leg (telemetry-timing reconciliation per JPL D-22027) is robust and closes the rescue independently regardless of how a defender reads the first leg. The argument is double-locked. No hole. (f) Citation spot-check: JPL D-22027 link still resolves to a real GRACE Level-1A telemetry memo on the podaac-tools subdomain; the backup PO.DAAC dataset page (GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03) is a legitimate dataset record; Pulkkinen et al. 2005 (doi.org/10.1029/2005JA011090) is the well-known Halloween-storms paper. (g) No new holes at major or moderate severity emerged from this pass.

citations_verified: Pulkkinen et al. 2005, doi.org/10.1029/2005JA011090 (Halloween storms paper)

advocate_mode_summary: Best case for the dome's chart: 'The BOU panel is real, the storm was real, and the cavity-transduction constant κ has cross-WIN support (WIN-011/012/058b cluster). The Forensic Zoom is a visualization of the cluster's central number for a non-technical audience; you reviewers are nit-picking labels.' Why this fails: (i) the cluster does not have independent cross-WIN support — WIN-012 shows κ is undefined under the dome's own SG nulls, WIN-058b shows κ is not derived from dome geometry. WIN-070's chart is the dome's last-line-of-defense visual proof, and visual proofs of an undefined value cannot rescue the value. (ii) Labeling matters when the chart is being marketed as forensic evidence — 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' is the chart's own headline, not a reviewer's reinterpretation. (iii) Calling the missing code 'a visualization, not a verification' concedes the falsifiability point: a visualization of a number computed nowhere is not evidence for that number.
cross_win_consistency_summary: Triangulated cleanly with WIN-012 (κ self-contradicted via WIN-013/014 SG nulls) and WIN-058b (κ not derived from dome geometry, r=0.992 unremarkable under shared-storm forcing). The three WINs target three different production stages of the same κ claim (derivation, internal consistency, public presentation) and do not contradict each other. The detail_extra 'Cross-WIN structure' paragraph now explicitly enumerates this triangulation.
quantitative_verification_summary: GRACE orbital parameters used in point (3): altitude ~500 km, speed ~7.5 km/s → ground track ~12,150 km over 27 minutes (= 7.5 × 60 × 27 = 12,150). Direct check ✓. The ~2-minute overhead window for spatial co-location is a Keplerian first-order estimate consistent with low-Earth-orbit pass geometry; the chart depicts 27 minutes, well outside any single overhead pass. ACC1A 10-Hz native sampling (JPL D-22027 §3) verified. Dst = −383 nT for Oct 30 2003 Halloween storm matches the World Data Centre Kyoto record. All quantitative figures consistent.
summary_for_decider: Clean verification. All three c3 minor carry-over holes (ISS-2083 spatial-temporal softening, ISS-2084 1-second-cadence reconciliation, ISS-2085 post_hoc_evidence annotation) verified closed against current data/wins.json:WIN-070. The three-cycle c1→c2→c3 post_hoc carry-over is retired. No new holes. recommended_action: no_change. The minor stylistic note about 'subset, not a prerequisite' in the patched first leg is documented in deeper_analysis (e) as non-blocking — the second leg robustly closes the rescue, so the argument is double-locked. WIN-070 c4 verification passed.
